segments.content: welcome to the master class on penic AI, a new framework for building AI agents. you may be familiar with penic, the typing and validation library for python. that is widely used for structuring input and output from llms. pyen AI is a new framework from the same team specifically designed for building agents, and that's what we'll be covering in this series. in this master class, you're going to learn about the core features of penti AI by building agents. we'll hit all key features of the framework with plenty of examples, so by the end of this, you'll have the confidence and knowledge to build your own agents. in this master class, we're going to code a lot of examples covering all critical aspects of the framework. we'll start simple by building our first agents in just five lines of code. next, we'll explore how to use different models, both commercial and also local open models such as llama 3.2 to build more capable agent systems gradually. we'll increase the complexity and capabilities of our agents by adding more features along the way. we'll explore what makes pantic AI unique among agent frameworks. speaking of unique, here are some of the features that make penic AI an attractive choice for building agent systems: it's built by the same team behind penic; it supports open AI, anthropic Gemini, and many more models; it seamlessly integrates with identic log fire for real-time debugging, performance monitoring, and tracking; it is type safe; it's designed to make type checking as easy as possible; it is python Centric; it leverages python for most operations; it uses the power of identic to validate and structure model outputs; it has a unique dependency injection system; and many more. we'll hit some of these unique features later in the presentation today. we'll take a high-level look at how penic AI works and how to create basic agents using open Ai olama and Azure open AI models, but before we start, let's define what penti AI is: it's a new AI agent framework from the same team that brought us penti; it supports many of the features of modern agent frameworks; and it aims to simplify the agent creation process. at the center of the flow is the agent as a python object; it makes calls to an llm; tools may be provided to enhance the context as well as static and dynamic system prompts; finally, runtime dependencies can help add dynamic context such as customer IDs or current weather conditions. penti AI uses plain python to control the flow of agent data, and there's no need for domain-specific code or extra classes. you can get more information by reading the wonderful documentation and exploring the examples at ai.penicdev. the framework leverages Python's control flow and agent composition to build any AI driven project making it easy to apply standard python best practices. you use or any AI or non-ai project. it is designed to make type checking as useful as possible, so it integrates well with static type checkers like mypy and pyite. it supports open AI, anthropic Gemini, olama Gro, and mistol, and there is a simple interface to implement support for other models. you can create multiple agents where each agent can operate with its own model exchanging information through message history and a dynamic runtime context. you can also harness the power of penic to validate and structure model outputs, ensuring responses are consistent across runs. it provides the ability to stream llm outputs continuously with immediate validation, ensuring rapid accurate results. it offers a dependency injection system unique among frameworks to provide data and services to your agent system prompts tools and results validators; this is useful for testing and eval-driven iterative development; you can inject critical context data such as customer info or the results of offline calculations during runtime through dynamic runtime context. it supports tools reflection and self-correction.

Penic AI is a new framework for building AI agents. It is specifically designed by the same team behind Penic, a typing and validation library for Python. This master class will cover the core features of Penic AI by building agents and hit all key features with plenty of examples. By the end of this master class, you'll have the confidence and knowledge to build your own agents. The framework leverages Python's control flow and agent composition to build any AI-driven project and supports open AI, Anthropic Gemini, and many more models. Penic AI uses plain Python to control the flow of agent data and integrates well with static type checkers like mypy and pyite.

Penci AI is a new AI agent framework from the same team that brought us Penic. It supports many of the features of modern agent frameworks and aims to simplify the agent creation process. At the center of the flow is the agent as a python object, it makes calls to an llm, tools may be provided to enhance the context as well as static and dynamic system prompts. Finally, runtime dependencies can help add dynamic context such as customer IDs or current weather conditions. Penci AI uses plain Python to control the flow of agent data, there's no need for domain-specific code or extra classes. You can get more information at ai.penic.dodev.

This section discusses the process of increasing agent complexity using the Penic AI framework. The goal is to build agents with more capabilities by utilizing various features provided by Penic AI. Some unique features include its integration with open AI, Anthropic Gemini, and many other models, seamless integration with Logfire for real-time debugging, performance monitoring, and tracking, type safety, Python Centric operation, validation, dependency injection system, and the ability to stream LLM outputs continuously with immediate validation. Users can create multiple agents, exchange information through message history, and inject critical context data during runtime through dynamic runtime context.

Penic AI is a new framework for building AI agents. It is specifically designed by the same team behind Pyen, a typing and validation library widely used for structuring input and output from LLMs. Here are some unique features that make Penic AI attractive for building agent systems: it supports open AI, Anthropic Gemini, and many more models; it seamlessly integrates with identic log fire for real-time debugging, performance monitoring, and tracking; it is type safe and designed to make type checking as easy as possible; it leverages Python for most operations; it uses the power of identic to validate and structure model outputs; it has a unique dependency injection system; it offers a dependency injection system unique among Frameworks to provide data and services to your agent system prompts tools and results validators; this is useful for testing and eval-driven iterative development; you can inject critical context data such as customer info or the results of offline calculations during runtime through Dynamic runtime context; it supports tools reflection and self-correction. The framework uses plain Python to control the flow of agent data, making it easy to apply standard Python best practices.

Penic AI is a new framework for building AI agents from the same team that developed the typing and validation library for Python called Penic. It supports open AI, Anthropic Gemini, and many more models. It seamlessly integrates with Identic Log Fire for real-time debugging, performance monitoring, and tracking. It is type safe, designed to make type checking as easy as possible, and leverages Python for most operations. It uses the power of Identic to validate and structure model outputs. It has a unique dependency injection system and many more features. In this master class, you will code examples covering all critical aspects of the framework, starting with building your first agents in just five lines of code. You'll explore how to use different models both commercial and local open models such as Llama 3.2 to build more capable agent systems gradually by increasing complexity and capabilities of your agents over time.
